name: phpMyFAQ
what_it_does: Self-hosted, multilingual FAQ and knowledge-base web application for PHP 8.4+ with enterprise auth (LDAP, Active Directory), full-text search via Elasticsearch/OpenSearch, REST API, and support for 40+ languages.
use_cases: Internal company knowledge base or support FAQ portal
Customer-facing self-service FAQ site with multilingual support
Integrating structured FAQ content into AI pipelines via REST API
Enterprise knowledge management with LDAP/Active Directory SSO
Documentation portals requiring revision history and WYSIWYG editing
not_for: Real-time collaborative wikis (use Confluence or MediaWiki instead)
best_when: You need a structured, searchable FAQ system with enterprise auth, multilingual support, and a REST API for programmatic access — and you want full control over hosting.
avoid_when: You need a hosted SaaS solution or a full wiki/CMS rather than a dedicated FAQ platform.

auth.notes: Supports local accounts, LDAP, Active Directory, Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), and two-factor authentication. Flexible per-user permission management.

pricing.notes: Mozilla Public License 2.0. Self-hosted; costs limited to hosting infrastructure.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard PHP/REST error responses; no MCP-specific error handling
known_agent_gotchas: No native MCP server — agents must use the REST API with OpenAPI 3.0 spec
Requires self-hosted PHP 8.4+ environment; no SaaS option
Elasticsearch/OpenSearch is optional but required for full search performance at scale

security_score_components.security_notes: Open-source FAQ platform. API key or JWT auth. Self-hosted — full control. SQL injection history in older versions — keep updated. CSRF protection built-in.
